# Weather forecasting model
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F

class WeatherNet(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_channels=2, hidden_channels=64, forecast_steps=12):
        super(WeatherNet, self).__init__()
        
        # Encoder for processing input sequence
        self.encoder = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(input_channels, hidden_channels, kernel_size=3, padding=1),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Conv2d(hidden_channels, hidden_channels, kernel_size=3, padding=1),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Conv2d(hidden_channels, hidden_channels, kernel_size=3, padding=1),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
        )
        
        # Temporal processing - LSTM to capture temporal dependencies
        self.temporal_conv = nn.Conv1d(hidden_channels, hidden_channels, kernel_size=3, padding=1)
        
        # Decoder for forecasting
        self.decoder = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(hidden_channels, hidden_channels, kernel_size=3, padding=1),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Conv2d(hidden_channels, hidden_channels, kernel_size=3, padding=1),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Conv2d(hidden_channels, input_channels, kernel_size=3, padding=1),
        )
        
        # Forecast steps parameter
        self.forecast_steps = forecast_steps
        
    def forward(self, x):
        # x shape: [batch, seq_len, channels, height, width]
        batch_size, seq_len, channels, height, width = x.size()
        
        # Process each timestep through encoder
        encoded_frames = []
        for i in range(seq_len):
            frame = x[:, i]  # [batch, channels, height, width]
            encoded_frame = self.encoder(frame)  # [batch, hidden_channels, height, width]
            encoded_frames.append(encoded_frame)
        
        # Stack encoded frames: [batch, seq_len, hidden_channels, height, width]
        encoded_seq = torch.stack(encoded_frames, dim=1)
        
        # Process temporal dimension
        # Reshape to [batch, hidden_channels, seq_len, height*width]
        reshaped = encoded_seq.permute(0, 2, 1, 3, 4).contiguous().view(batch_size, -1, seq_len, height*width)
        
        # Apply temporal convolution
        temporal_out = self.temporal_conv(reshaped.view(batch_size, -1, seq_len))
        
        # Reshape back to spatial format
        temporal_out = temporal_out.view(batch_size, -1, height, width)
        
        # Decode to get final prediction
        output = self.decoder(temporal_out)
        
        return output
